CLAUDE.md

Source: CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

Project Overview

marlin is an R package for spatially explicit simulation of marine fauna and fisheries. It runs multiple independent age-structured species simultaneously on 2D spatial grids with fishing fleets, management strategies (MPAs, quotas), and complex life histories. Uses R6 classes, Rcpp/RcppEigen for performance-critical C++ code.

Build & Development Commands

# Install package (from R or terminal)
R CMD INSTALL .

# Or from R console with devtools:
# devtools::install()
# devtools::load_all()   # load without installing (preferred during dev)

# Rebuild documentation (Roxygen2)
# devtools::document()

# Full R CMD check
R CMD check .

# Run tests (testthat)
# devtools::test()
# devtools::test(filter = "basic-simulation")  # run a single test file

# Build pkgdown site
# pkgdown::build_site()

After modifying C++ files in src/, you must recompile (devtools::load_all() or R CMD INSTALL .).

Architecture

Core Simulation Loop

simmar() (R/simmar.R) is the main entry point. Each time step: 1. Compute effort dynamics (constant/open-access/sole-owner/manual) 2. Spatially allocate effort across patches 3. Call C++ sim_fish() for population dynamics (Baranov catch equation, mortality, movement, recruitment) 4. Account for yields (catch, revenue, profit, CPUE) 5. Enforce quotas if applicable

R6 Classes

  • Fish (R/fish_class.R) — Age-structured population with spatial grid, life history params, movement matrices (sparse column-stochastic), density dependence
  • Metier (R/metier_class.R) — Fleet-species interaction: selectivity, catchability, price

Factory Functions

  • create_critter() (R/create_critter.R) — Builds Fish objects with optional FishLife species lookup (query_fishlife param), handles burn-in to unfished equilibrium. Life history params (linf, vbk, m, weight_a, weight_b, age_mature, etc.) are named parameters; when NA and query_fishlife = TRUE, they are filled from FishLife. User-supplied values always take precedence.
  • create_fleet() (R/create_fleet.R) — Builds fleet objects with metier list, fleet model (constant_effort/open_access/sole_owner/manual), spatial allocation strategy
  • tune_fleets() (R/tune_fleets.R) — Calibrates catchability via optimization to achieve target depletion/F/catch

C++ Core (src/)

  • sim_fish.cpp — Population dynamics engine (Baranov, BH recruitment, movement via Eigen sparse matrices)
  • ifd_rcpp.cpp — Ideal Free Distribution spatial effort allocation (KKT solver)
  • cpp-helpers.cpp — Utility functions
  • All compiled via Rcpp/RcppEigen; shared object is marlin.so

Output Processing

  • process_marlin() (R/process_marlin.R) — Converts nested list output to data.table
  • plot_marlin() (R/plot_marlin.R) — ggplot2 visualization of biomass, catch, SSB over space/time

Typical Workflow

fauna <- list(species1 = create_critter(...))
fleets <- list(fleet1 = create_fleet(..., metiers = list(species1 = Metier$new(...))))
fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets, ...)
sim <- simmar(fauna, fleets, years = 20)
processed <- process_marlin(sim)
plot_marlin(processed)

Key Conventions

  • NAMESPACE uses exportPattern("^[[:alpha:]]+") — all public R functions starting with a letter are exported
  • Documentation generated with Roxygen2 (@param, @return, @export tags in R files)
  • Movement matrices are sparse (via sparsify_movement()) for memory/speed
  • Requires R >= 4.1.0; needs Rcpp and RcppEigen compilation toolchain
  • bold and taxize packages installed from GitHub (ropensci/bold, ropensci/taxize)

On this page

Developed by Dan Ovando.
